summary: Test suite provides well-structured parametrized integration tests covering the happy path: feat/fix/breaking version bumps, version file updates, changelog generation, and workflow structure validation. Workflow tests (actionlint, YAML parse, file references) are thorough and inexpensive. However, critical gaps exist: zero error scenario testing (malformed input, missing files, invalid JSON, empty commits), no boundary condition testing (edge case semver values), no edge case variation (BREAKING CHANGE footer vs ! marker, plain text VERSION files, pre-existing changelog handling, whitespace normalization). Design is excellent for parametrized cases (clear CASES list, good isolation via tmp_path, readable helpers) but heavyweight and slow (requires Docker/git/act per case). No unit tests for pure functions shown—all testing through act integration layer. Would reliably catch regressions in main bumping logic but miss error handling, alternative formats, and edge cases. Missing root-level artifacts (workflow file, fixtures/package.json) block independent verification.
